school: Madina School — niece and student of Aisha bint Abi Bakr; Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz ordered hadiths to be collected on her authority; overruled a judge's ruling on grounds of prophetic law

books.title: Hadith transmission — authoritative on Aisha's legal rulings

school: Medinan Quranic School — one of the seven canonical qaris; his riwaya dominates in North and West Africa today

books.title: Wikipedia — Nafi' al-Madani
books.note: His recitation style (riwaya of Warsh and Qalun) remains dominant in North/West Africa

books.title: Sirat Rasul Allah (Life of the Prophet)

school: The foremost hadith scholar of the Hijaz; the Prophet said in a hadith: 'Seek knowledge from four' — and he was one of them; al-Shafi'i said he learned Quran exegesis from Sufyan ibn Uyayna; known for his remarkable memory retaining 9,000 hadiths; also an important link in Sufi chains via his knowledge of inner meanings

books.title: al-Tafsir (Quran Commentary — transmitted fragments)
books.note: His Quranic interpretations are cited throughout classical tafsir

school: The founding father of systematic hadith criticism (al-jarh wa'l-ta'dil — impugning and accrediting narrators); Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: 'I have not seen anyone like Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan'; his rigorous evaluation of hadith transmitters became the methodological standard for all subsequent hadith scholarship; without him the Six Books may never have been compiled

books.title: al-Du'afa' (Weak Transmitters — preserved through students)
books.note: Foundational work in hadith narrator criticism methodology

school: Combined the life of scholar and warrior; spent summers on military campaigns and winters teaching hadith; his poem to a friend in Baghdad asking 'why are you worshipping in a mosque while we fight on the frontier?' is a classic of Islamic ascetic literature; Ahmad ibn Hanbal wept when he died; founder of Khorasani hadith tradition

books.title: Kitab al-Jihad (Book of Holy War)
books.note: One of the earliest works specifically on Islamic ethics of warfare

school: The peer of Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan in hadith criticism; al-Shafi'i dedicated his book al-Risala to him; when Ahmad ibn Hanbal was asked about him he said: 'His knowledge is comprehensive and his piety complete'; established the methodology for evaluating chains of transmission that every subsequent muhaddith followed

books.title: al-Ilal fi'l-Hadith (Hadith Defects — transmitted through students)
books.note: Pioneered the science of identifying hidden defects in hadith chains

children: Ibrahim bin 'Abdur Rahman , Musa bin 'Abdur Rahman bin Mhda

school: Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: 'I have not seen anyone more complete in knowledge than Waki' ibn al-Jarrah'; both al-Shafi'i and Ahmad ibn Hanbal studied under him; famous for combining exceptional memory with piety; al-Shafi'i composed a poem about him after he complained of forgetting things

books.title: al-Zuhd (Asceticism — partially preserved)
books.note: One of the early texts on Islamic asceticism

children: Sufyan bin Wky' bin al-Jrah , Mlyh bin Wky' bin al-Jrah , 'Ubaid bin Wky' bin al-Jrah

school: Khorasan / Hadith Sciences — The gold standard of hadith authentication

books.title: Sahih al-Bukhari (The Authentic Collection)
books.note: Most authentic book after the Quran according to Sunni Muslims — 7,563 narrations selected from 600,000.

school: Khorasan / Hadith Sciences — Second most authoritative hadith collection

books.title: Sahih Muslim (The Authentic Collection)
books.note: Second most authoritative hadith collection in Sunni Islam — 7,500 narrations with rigorous chain analysis.

school: Basran / Hadith Sciences — Focus on legal narrations

books.title: Sunan Abu Dawud
books.note: One of the six canonical hadith collections; focus on legal narrations — Ahmad ibn Hanbal praised it as sufficient for religion.

school: Khorasan School / Hadith Sciences — studied under Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal and al-Bukhari

books.title: Sahih Muslim (3,033 hadiths — most authentic after Bukhari)
books.note: Sahih Muslim is the second most authoritative hadith collection; studied under Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

school: Khorasan / Hadith Sciences — Unique for including legal opinions of early jurists alongside narrations

books.title: Jami al-Tirmidhi (Al-Sunan)
books.note: One of the six canonical hadith collections; unique for including legal opinions of early jurists.

school: Khorasan / Hadith Sciences — Sixth and final of the six canonical hadith collections

books.title: Sunan Ibn Majah
books.note: The sixth of the six canonical hadith collections in Sunni Islam.

school: Khorasan / Egypt — Hadith Sciences; known for strictest authentication criteria

books.title: Al-Sunan al-Kubra / Al-Mujtaba
books.note: Known for the strictest hadith criteria among the six canonical collections.

school: Shia Imami Tradition
Qum School of Hadith
Hadith transmission — the most celebrated female hadith scholar of Baghdad; students came from across the Islamic world

books.title: Hadith certificates (ijazas)
books.url: ,note:"Renowned for rare hadith chains — gave ijaza to scholars across the world

school: Shafi'i school — greatest hadith scholar of his era; wrote the 40 Hadith al-Nawawi still memorized worldwide

books.title: Forty Hadith (Al-Arba'un al-Nawawiyya)
books.url: https://archive.org/details/FortyHadithAlNawawi",note:"The most widely memorized hadith collection in Islamic history

books.title: Riyadh al-Salihin
books.url: https://archive.org/details/RiyadhAsSaliheenByImamNawawi",note:"Gardens of the Righteous — globally used hadith collection

books.title: A'azz ma Yutlab
books.url: ,note:"Almohad creed — foundational text of the Almohad reform movement

school: Hadith transmission — the most prolific female hadith scholar of medieval Damascus; Ibn Hajar was among her students

books.title: Hadith musnad collections
books.url: ,note:"Had the highest chains of transmission in Syria — students made pilgrimages to hear her

school: Shafi'i / Damascene school — Wrote the most comprehensive biography of Islamic scholars across 23 volumes

books.title: Siyar A'lam al-Nubala (Lives of Noble Figures — 23 vols)
books.note: Most comprehensive biographical dictionary in Islamic history — over 5,000 scholars profiled.

school: Shafi'i school — student of Ibn Taymiyya; wrote the definitive classical tafsir

books.title: Tafsir Ibn Kathir
books.url: https://archive.org/details/TafsirIbnKathir",note:"The most widely used classical Quranic commentary in the world

school: Shafi'i / Egyptian Hadith Sciences — Last great classical hadith master; his Bukhari commentary defines the science

books.title: Fath al-Bari (Commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari — 13 vols)
books.note: Greatest commentary ever written on Sahih al-Bukhari — the definitive reference for all hadith scholarship.

books.title: Al-Isaba fi Tamyiz al-Sahaba (Companions of the Prophet — biographies)

school: Shafi'i school — major biographer and hadith master of the 15th century

books.title: Al-Daw' al-Lami'
books.url: ,note:"12-volume biographical dictionary of 10,000 scholars of his era
